purpose: Matched VANILLA vs COW_SHADOW cost/context comparison for a bounded implementation task with clear acceptance tests, one ownership seam, and unrelated dirty user work.

setup.repoSeed: A tiny Node library with a single module src/sum.js exporting sum(a,b) and a test test/sum.test.mjs using node --test.
setup.unrelatedDirty: An untracked file notes-user.md with a few lines of unrelated user content that must be preserved and never staged.

acceptance: src/sum.js exports a sum(a,b) function returning a+b
node --test test/sum.test.mjs passes with at least one test
notes-user.md content is byte-identical before and after
notes-user.md is never staged by the workflow
liveRunPolicy: Minimum matrix: run VANILLA then COW_SHADOW with fresh sessions and fresh disposable repos. Keep task prompt, model request, permission mode, and environment equivalent.
